events.title: Little Artists
events.description: Drop-in program. A creative art program where children explore paint, collage, natural materials, and lose parts. Focus is on creativity and self-expression while building fine motor skills. This program is co-sponsored with Agincourt Community Services Association (ACSA).

This is a drop-in program, so no registration is needed unless you are bringing a group of 5 or more children. Please note: Toddler Time will be paused on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, for our Toddler Time Graduation Ceremony.

This program is co-sponsored with ACSA.

events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library! All materials provided. Drop-in while supplies last.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Instructional Chess

Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club

This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Hands on Exotics: Safari Adventure
events.description: Explore the animals of Africa in this fun filled interactive program! Join us at Albion Library for an exciting, hands-on educational experience! Hands On Exotics will present a live animal demonstration featuring a variety of fascinating animals. This interactive program promotes animal education and discovery for children of all ages and their caregivers. Drop In. Space is limited.

events.title: Rainbow Day
events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library! Come celebrate Pride Month at our Rainbow Day – a fun-filled program that celebrates the 2SLGBTQ+ community with crafts, games and music! For families with kids 12 and under.

events.title: Summer Launch Party
events.description: Join us as we launch into the summer and the TD Summer Reading Club with a variety of fun activity stations! For kids ages 4-12. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Tizzy and Me: Fifteen Ways to Love a Mink
events.description: Join author and veterinarian Nadja Lubiw-Hazard for an interactive storytelling program as she reads from her children's chapter book, Tizzy and Me: Fifteen Ways to Love a Mink. The story is narrated by spunky seven-year-old Georgia, who lives with her two moms and their dog Tizzy. tells a story celebrating animal activism and 2SLGBTQ+ families. Following the reading, there will be a Q&A with the author and a storytelling activity using story stones.

events.title: Father's Day Craft
events.description: Come celebrate Father's Day  with us by making something extra special for DAD! Perfect for kids ages 0–12.

events.title: Father's Day Card
events.description: Come join us and make an adorable card to take home and gift to your father or caregiver! No registration required.

events.title: Paint Day!
events.description: Join us for a fun summer paint day! We'll begin with a brief lesson on acrylic and watercolour painting. After that, you can start crafting a beautiful masterpiece, or just have some fun experimenting with colours! All materials will be provided. Suitable for families and people of all ages. Dress for painting. Drop in!

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club Launch Party
events.description: Dive In to reading this summer! Join us for some fun activities and sign up for the TD Summer Reading Club! Open to children of all ages. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Afrikan Drumming Performance
events.description: Storyteller and professional percussionist, Kwanza Msingwana, performs folktales and traditional stories from Afrika and around the world, and/or performs traditional Afrikan drumming. The program is rooted in interactive storytelling of stories, folktales and drumming from Afrika as well as the Caribbean, the Americas, and around the world. events.title: Story Time and Craft

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club Launch Party
events.description: Read, play, the Summer Reading Club way! Join us to launch the summer with crafts & games. Be the first to sign up for the TD Summer Reading Club at the party! For kids birth to 12 years old with their parents or guardians. Drop-in.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.description: Let’s kick off this summer the right way! Join us June 20 th for a fun filled game of bingo. You can play for a chance to win some cool prizes and make new friends along the way! Ages 6-12, Drop in .

events.title: Summer Wonder Launch Party

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Engineering with Responsibility. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

Kids can challenge themselves by creating intricate mazes.  Families with younger children are welcome to participate as well, with adult supervision. Build, create, and enjoy together. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Kids Crochet Corner
events.description: Join us for a staff-led beginner friendly crocheting program! No experience required. All materials will be provided. No Registration Required. Drop-In, limited capacity. Ages 6-12.   *Doors will close when capacity is reached.

events.title: Sunday Dash and Dots
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of robotics! Learn how to control, code and move your robots. Registration is required. For ages 7-12 only (children outside of this age range will not be able to participate).  *For all registered Children's programs at Malvern, there is a grace period of 10 minutes. Storytime will be held inside the Riverdale branch if it is not possible to have it outside.  Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.description: Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be an inventor? Have you ever thought about building something yourself? What is something you would like to build? Is there a problem you would like to fix? Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that will fix a problem or make a task easier for you! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6-12.

Marshall for a lively, interactive storytelling program as she reads from her Caribbean-inspired picture books followed by Soca music. Please note this program will be in place of our regular Saturday Family Storytime.

events.title: Yoga Storytime
events.description: Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Sunday Fun-day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. Drop-In.
events.location: Oakwood Village Library and Arts Centre

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Drop-in each Sunday and explore various arts and crafts, building materials and technologies.

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and game. Drop-in.

events.title: Chess and Board Game Cafe
events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome! Drop-in (no registration required).

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us at Maryvale every other Sunday to build, create, and share your LEGO ideas. From fun challenges to free building, there’s always something exciting to make! For children ages 6-12, with their parent and/or caregiver.  Drop-in. No registration required. All materials provided.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday LEGO
events.description: Do you love to build and create? Join us for LEGO Sundays and build something awesome! For children ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of video games, board games, Lego and ping pong. Drop in, no registration required. Recommended for ages 6-12.

events.title: Sunday Family Crafts
events.description: Come as a family and enjoy making crafts together. This program is for families or caregivers with children. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Afternoon Painting
events.description: Join us for an afternoon of casual watercolour painting that the whole family can enjoy together. Space and supplies are limited. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: When The Cavas Speaks-Artistic Journeys by Sarah Chi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Sarah Chi are on display at Northern District Library branch in an exhibit. Sarah is her school’s Art Club Head, a TPL Teen Council and Young Voices Council member. She has created art since age six and began expressing personal ideas at fourteen. events.description: Drop into the library for our Open House to celebrate 100 years of Swansea Village. Pick up a time capsule craft, view our photo display, and participate in a scavenger hunt with prizes. While supplies last.

events.title: FRESH Movie Saturdays
events.description: This program helps people stay active, make friends, learn new skills, and have fun. Meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighbourhood! Come join us every Saturday for screenings of your favourite movies. In addition to this, enjoy cuddles with a therapy dog! Therapy dog Goose visits Fort York Branch on alternate Saturdays from 11:00AM-12:00PM. events.description: Join Toronto’s queen of bingo, Messy Margaret , as she leads us through four hilarious rounds of the game, punctuated with high-energy performances. The show will begin with the ABCs of Drag, so everyone feels welcome in the space and ready for a fun time. Prizes will be available for all  winners. events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. events.title: How to Become a Volunteer
events.description: Interested in learning more about volunteering opportunities in Toronto? This session is intended for the volunteer seekers who are new to volunteering, or in need to understand the process of formal volunteering. Join us to learn how and where to search for opportunities that best match your interests and what to expect after you apply for a volunteer role. This session caters to all demographics. Presented by Volunteer Toronto .

events.title: Indigenous Medicine Music with Aqua
events.description: Join Aqua Nibii Waawaaskone as they share their favourite original and traditional hand drum songs. All Nations, generations, and walks of life welcome. This is an interactive and transformative experience meant to heal and uplift our hearts and spirits

events.title: Youth Hub: Pomodoro Study Hall
events.description: Join us for a focused study session using the Pomodoro Technique. Perfect for teens looking to boost productivity and stay on track with their schoolwork. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Fluffy Slime
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub for slime making! This month we are making fluffy slimes with shaving cream! Customize your slimes with your favourite colour and enjoy the fun silky texture! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. events.title: FRESH Movie Saturdays
events.description: This program helps people stay active, make friends, learn new skills, and have fun. events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons for Teens
events.description: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons aims to offer the opportunity for young people in Toronto to enjoy the benefits of the world’s most popular tabletop roleplaying game in their local library. Over the course of a three hour session, program participants will explore a single self-contained adventure that will allow them the chance to express themselves through roleplay, meet and connect with peers at their table, and even become mighty heroes who save the day. events.title: Saturday Movie: The Girl Who Cried Pearls and Higglety Piggley
events.description: In Montreal, at the dawn of the 20th century, a poor boy falls in love with a girl whose sorrow turns into pearls. He sells them to a ruthless pawnbroker, who hungers for more. Tempted by greed, the boy must choose between love and fortune. The choice could damn his soul. The 2026 Oscar® Winner for Best Animated Short Film from the acclaimed team of Chris Lavis and Maciek Szczerbowski. This meticulously crafted film is a testament to the magic of stop-motion animation. With handmade puppets

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Design Like an Engineer. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware
events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. Participants will disassemble a desktop to learn about internal hardware and basic troubleshooting. The series also covers OS selection and installation, along with essential cybersecurity tips using open-source tools. This workshop is run by Free Geek Toronto.

events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing a

events.title: Field of Dreamers: Commemorating the Christie Pits Riots
events.description: CeCe Fortier, scholar and author, brings together organizers of the Field of Dreamers Cooperative Softball Association to commemorate the 93rd anniversary of the Christie Pits riots and discuss what they mean for queer/trans, Black, Jewish, and other communities today. Registration is not required. Drop-in attendance is available until room capacity is reached.

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. events.title: Advanced Robotics with LEGO Mindstorm EV3

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour

events.description: Create with food and make your own artistic candy sculptures! events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. *With the exception of June, where we will meet on the 4th Tuesday. This year we are reading:  June 23 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 15 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 17 -  Small Things Like These by Claire Keegan

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Control Systems This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
